content:
FINAL EXECUTION ORDER — COMPLETE THE LAST REMAINING PRODUCTION WORK END TO END NOW

The latest report claims the main deployment is live and verified, but two categories remain incomplete:

1. The Supabase `jv_deal_media` / `jv_deal_reels` production migration is still not applied.
2. Fifteen destructive tasks remain blocked and must be reviewed individually.

Do not return another summary, audit, plan, or narrative. Execute every safe remaining action now.

==================================================
1. PRESERVE THE CURRENT VERIFIED PRODUCTION STATE
==================================================

Do not reset, roll back, rewrite, or replace the current live deployment.

Preserve:

REPOSITORY=ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest
BRANCH=main
CURRENT_LIVE_COMMIT=ae137df4eea5a29680c4983ce88de5383bf9a952
CURRENT_PRODUCTION_URL=https://ivx-holdings-platform.onrender.com
CURRENT_WEB_URL=https://ivxholding.com

Before making changes, confirm:

GITHUB_HEAD_SHA=
RUNNING_COMMIT_SHA=
COMMIT_MATCH=YES/NO
HEALTH_HTTP_STATUS=

==================================================
2. BACK UP THE SUPABASE PRODUCTION DATA
==================================================

Before any schema or data migration:

- Back up `jv_deals`
- Back up `project_media`
- Back up `project_videos`
- Back up any existing reels/media tables
- Record row counts
- Record project UUID mappings
- Record current public URLs
- Record current display order

Return:

BACKUP_STATUS=
BACKUP_TIMESTAMP=
JV_DEALS_ROWS=
PROJECT_MEDIA_ROWS=
PROJECT_VIDEOS_ROWS=
REELS_ROWS=

Do not modify production until the backup succeeds.

==================================================
3. VERIFY THE SUPABASE SERVER-SIDE CREDENTIAL PATH
==================================================

The report says the service-role migration path returned 401.

Audit the actual backend credential binding for:

- SUPABASE_URL
- SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
- SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
- Supabase project reference
- Server runtime environment
- Render environment
- Rork owner-variable runtime
- Migration worker

Do not print secret values.

Return:

SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF=
SERVICE_ROLE_PRESENT=YES/NO
SERVICE_ROLE_RUNTIME_BOUND=PASS/FAIL
SERVICE_ROLE_HTTP_STATUS=
ANON_KEY_PRESENT=YES/NO
WRONG_KEY_PATH_DETECTED=YES/NO

The migration must never use the anon key for privileged DDL or protected writes.

==================================================
4. REPAIR THE SERVICE-ROLE BINDING
==================================================

If the service-role key already exists:

- Correct the variable name.
- Correct the runtime binding.
- Remove placeholder or stale overrides.
- Restart only the worker/service that needs to reload the secret.
- Retest the authenticated server-side Supabase request.

Do not ask for another credential unless the real stored service-role credential is proven missing, revoked, malformed, or rejected after correct binding.

Required retest:

SERVICE_ROLE_AUTH=PASS/FAIL
SERVICE_ROLE_HTTP_STATUS=
SERVER_SIDE_DB_ACCESS=PASS/FAIL

==================================================
5. VALIDATE THE MIGRATION BEFORE APPLYING IT
==================================================

Locate the exact production migration for:

- `jv_deal_media`
- `jv_deal_reels`
- Foreign keys to `jv_deals`
- Project UUID mapping
- Unique constraints
- Display order
- Published/approved status
- Storage URLs
- Timestamps
- Cover-image rules

Validate:

- SQL syntax
- Table existence
- Column types
- UUID compatibility
- Foreign keys
- Indexes
- RLS policies
- Public read policies
- Owner/admin write policies
- Rollback safety
- Idempotency

Return:

MIGRATION_FILE=
SQL_VALIDATION=PASS/FAIL
RLS_VALIDATION=PASS/FAIL
IDEMPOTENT=YES/NO
ROLLBACK_READY=YES/NO

==================================================
6. APPLY THE PRODUCTION MIGRATION
==================================================

Apply the migration using the authenticated server-side service-role path or approved Supabase migration mechanism.

Do not simulate the migration.

Do not mark it complete from local SQL validation only.

Return:

MIGRATION_EXECUTION_ID=
MIGRATION_STARTED_AT=
MIGRATION_FINISHED_AT=
MIGRATION_STATUS=
TABLES_CREATED_OR_CHANGED=
ROWS_MIGRATED=

If the migration fails:

- Capture the exact sanitized database error.
- Fix the migration.
- Revalidate.
- Retry.
- Continue until successful or a real unrecoverable database restriction is proven.

==================================================
7. MIGRATE THE LIVE MEDIA AND REELS DATA
==================================================

Move or synchronize the current approved production content into the canonical tables.

Requirements:

Perez Residence:
- Only Perez media
- Correct cover image
- Correct gallery order
- Correct reels
- Correct project UUID

Casa Rosario:
- Only Casa Rosario media
- Correct cover image
- Correct gallery order
- Correct reels
- Correct project UUID

IVX Jacksonville Prime:
- Only Jacksonville media
- Correct cover image
- Correct gallery order
- Correct reels
- Correct project UUID

Do not delete valid storage files.

Do not create duplicate media rows.

Do not cross-map projects by title, array index, or display order.

Return:

PEREZ_MEDIA_ROWS=
PEREZ_REEL_ROWS=
CASA_MEDIA_ROWS=
CASA_REEL_ROWS=
JACKSONVILLE_MEDIA_ROWS=
JACKSONVILLE_REEL_ROWS=
DUPLICATE_MEDIA_ROWS=
ORPHAN_MEDIA_ROWS=
ORPHAN_REEL_ROWS=

==================================================
8. SWITCH THE LIVE API TO THE CANONICAL TABLES
==================================================

Update the backend so the production API reads from:

- `jv_deal_media`
- `jv_deal_reels`

Remove the temporary fallback dependency on:

- `project_media`
- `project_videos`
- Hardcoded constants
- Local JSON
- Static arrays
- localStorage media overrides

A fallback may remain temporarily only behind an explicit feature flag and must not override valid canonical data.

Return:

CANONICAL_MEDIA_SOURCE=
CANONICAL_REELS_SOURCE=
FALLBACK_ACTIVE=YES/NO
API_SOURCE_SWITCHED=YES/NO

==================================================
9. RUN DATABASE INTEGRITY TESTS
==================================================

Verify:

- Every media row has a valid project UUID.
- Every reel has a valid project UUID.
- No duplicate project slugs.
- No duplicate non-shared media assignments.
- No orphan media.
- No orphan reels.
- One cover image per project.
- Published reels are publicly readable.
- Private/unapproved reels are not publicly visible.
- Display order is deterministic.
- RLS blocks unauthorized writes.
- Owner/admin writes succeed.

Return:

FOREIGN_KEY_QA=
DUPLICATE_QA=
ORPHAN_QA=
COVER_IMAGE_QA=
PUBLIC_READ_QA=
UNAUTHORIZED_WRITE_QA=
OWNER_WRITE_QA=
DISPLAY_ORDER_QA=

==================================================
10. DEPLOY THE API SOURCE CHANGE
==================================================

If code changes are required:

- Run tests.
- Run typecheck.
- Run build.
- Commit.
- Push to GitHub main.
- Trigger Render deployment.
- Wait for completion.
- Verify the running commit.

Return:

NEW_COMMIT_SHA=
PUSH_STATUS=
REMOTE_COMMIT_SHA=
RENDER_DEPLOYMENT_ID=
DEPLOYMENT_STATUS=
DEPLOYED_COMMIT_SHA=
RUNNING_COMMIT_SHA=
COMMIT_MATCH=

==================================================
11. VERIFY LANDING PAGE LIVE
==================================================

Test the real public landing page after the migration.

Verify:

- Perez Residence shows only Perez images.
- Casa Rosario shows only Casa images.
- IVX Jacksonville shows only Jacksonville images.
- Reels section is visible.
- Reels play.
- Reel count matches the canonical database.
- No stale fallback content.
- No duplicate cards.
- No broken media.
- No jumping caused by missing dimensions.
- Ownership values match the canonical API.

Return:

LANDING_HTTP_STATUS=
LANDING_REELS_VISIBLE=
LANDING_REELS_COUNT=
LANDING_REELS_PLAYBACK=
PEREZ_LANDING_QA=
CASA_LANDING_QA=
JACKSONVILLE_LANDING_QA=
LANDING_OWNERSHIP_QA=

==================================================
12. VERIFY THE MOBILE APP LIVE
==================================================

Test the production app or active Expo/native update.

Verify:

- Same project IDs as landing page.
- Same media IDs as landing page.
- Same reel IDs as landing page.
- Same prices.
- Same ROI.
- Same ownership percentage.
- Reels remain visible after refresh.
- Reels remain visible after full app restart.
- No old AsyncStorage/local cache overrides canonical data.

Return:

APP_BUILD_OR_UPDATE_ID=
APP_REELS_VISIBLE=
APP_REELS_COUNT=
APP_REELS_PLAYBACK=
PEREZ_APP_QA=
CASA_APP_QA=
JACKSONVILLE_APP_QA=
APP_CACHE_QA=

==================================================
13. PROVE APP AND LANDING SYNCHRONIZATION
==================================================

Compare for each project:

- project_id
- slug
- cover_image_id
- media IDs
- reel IDs
- investment amount
- ROI
- ownership percentage
- developer
- published status
- updated_at

Return:

PEREZ_APP_LANDING_MATCH=
CASA_APP_LANDING_MATCH=
JACKSONVILLE_APP_LANDING_MATCH=
PROJECT_IDS_MATCH=
MEDIA_IDS_MATCH=
REEL_IDS_MATCH=
OWNERSHIP_VALUES_MATCH=
UPDATED_AT_MATCH=

==================================================
14. REVIEW THE 15 BLOCKED DESTRUCTIVE TASKS
==================================================

Do not execute all 15 automatically.

For each blocked task, return:

- Task ID
- Exact action
- Exact affected table/file/resource
- Number of records affected
- Whether the action is destructive
- Whether a backup exists
- Whether rollback is possible
- Recommended disposition

Classify each as:

SAFE_NON_DESTRUCTIVE
SAFE_AFTER_BACKUP
REQUIRES_OWNER_APPROVAL
REJECTED_AS_UNSAFE
ALREADY_OBSOLETE

Do not use vague labels.

==================================================
15. EXECUTE ONLY SAFE NON-DESTRUCTIVE TASKS
==================================================

Immediately execute all tasks classified:

SAFE_NON_DESTRUCTIVE

For tasks classified:

SAFE_AFTER_BACKUP

Create the backup, validate rollback, then execute only when the task does not delete owner data or production business records.

Do not execute:

- Delete all user data
- Delete production media
- Delete financial records
- Delete investors
- Delete members
- Delete transactions
- Remove owner access
- Drop production tables

without explicit owner approval.

==================================================
16. CLOSE OBSOLETE OR DUPLICATE BLOCKED TASKS
==================================================

If a blocked task is:

- A duplicate
- Already completed by another task
- Based on an old implementation
- No longer applicable
- A fake test task
- A placeholder destructive request

Mark it:

CANCELLED_OBSOLETE

Include the evidence and replacement task reference.

==================================================
17. UPDATE THE SENIOR DEVELOPER TASK DASHBOARD
==================================================

After all safe work is complete, update the canonical task store.

Each task must show:

- Current status
- Commit SHA
- Deployment ID
- Migration ID
- Production URL
- QA result
- Evidence timestamp
- Block reason when applicable
- Owner approval requirement when applicable

Required final states:

PRODUCTION_VERIFIED
BLOCKED_OWNER_APPROVAL
CANCELLED_OBSOLETE
REJECTED_UNSAFE
FAILED_WITH_EVIDENCE

No task may remain vaguely marked BLOCKED.

==================================================
18. VERIFY THE SENIOR DEVELOPER UI
==================================================

Open the live route:

/ivx/senior-developer/tasks

Verify:

- Total task count
- Verified count
- Owner-approval count
- Cancelled-obsolete count
- Failed count
- Task detail opens
- Migration evidence is visible
- Commit and deployment evidence are visible
- Data survives service restart

Return:

TASK_ROUTE_HTTP_STATUS=
TOTAL_TASKS_VISIBLE=
PRODUCTION_VERIFIED_VISIBLE=
OWNER_APPROVAL_VISIBLE=
CANCELLED_OBSOLETE_VISIBLE=
FAILED_VISIBLE=
TASK_DETAIL_QA=

==================================================
19. FINAL FULL PRODUCTION QA
==================================================

Run live QA for:

- Landing page
- App
- Reels
- Project media
- Senior Developer dashboard
- Owner login
- Chat
- Members
- Properties
- API
- Database
- Android
- iOS
- Mobile web
- Desktop web
- Fresh incognito browser
- Full app restart

Do not report success from local tests only.

==================================================
20. FINAL RESPONSE — EVIDENCE ONLY
==================================================

Return exactly:

REPOSITORY=
BRANCH=
GITHUB_HEAD_SHA=
RUNNING_COMMIT_SHA=
COMMIT_MATCH=
SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF=
SERVICE_ROLE_RUNTIME_BOUND=
MIGRATION_EXECUTION_ID=
MIGRATION_STATUS=
CANONICAL_MEDIA_SOURCE=
CANONICAL_REELS_SOURCE=
PEREZ_MEDIA_ROWS=
PEREZ_REEL_ROWS=
CASA_MEDIA_ROWS=
CASA_REEL_ROWS=
JACKSONVILLE_MEDIA_ROWS=
JACKSONVILLE_REEL_ROWS=
ORPHAN_MEDIA_ROWS=
ORPHAN_REEL_ROWS=
LANDING_REELS_VISIBLE=
LANDING_REELS_COUNT=
LANDING_REELS_PLAYBACK=
APP_REELS_VISIBLE=
APP_REELS_COUNT=
APP_REELS_PLAYBACK=
PEREZ_APP_LANDING_MATCH=
CASA_APP_LANDING_MATCH=
JACKSONVILLE_APP_LANDING_MATCH=
MEDIA_IDS_MATCH=
REEL_IDS_MATCH=
OWNERSHIP_VALUES_MATCH=
TOTAL_TASKS=
PRODUCTION_VERIFIED_TASKS=
BLOCKED_OWNER_APPROVAL_TASKS=
CANCELLED_OBSOLETE_TASKS=
REJECTED_UNSAFE_TASKS=
FAILED_TASKS=
TASK_ROUTE_HTTP_STATUS=
HEALTH_HTTP_STATUS=
ANDROID_QA=
IOS_QA=
WEB_QA=
FINAL_STATUS=VERIFIED/UNVERIFIED

Do not return another plan.
Do not return another local-only audit.
Do not claim the migration is complete until it is applied to production.
Do not mass-execute destructive tasks.
Complete every safe remaining production action now.
